pageTimings.text: Luna woke up knowing she had forgotten something important. She sat on her branch. She thought very hard. She could not remember what it was.
This was the problem with forgetting things. You couldn't always remember what you had forgotten. Luna knew it was important. She knew it was today.
What do you remember about it? asked Pip. I remember it was today, said Luna. And it was important. And... she paused. I think it was outside.
Was it a meeting? asked Fern. I don't think so, said Luna. Was it something to collect? asked Burrow. Possibly, said Luna. I cannot quite say.
Everyone helped. They checked the Hollow Oak from top to bottom. They walked the paths. They looked in the Whispering Meadow. Nothing triggered the memory.
Was it something to do with the Crystal Stream? asked Dewdrop. Luna stopped walking. She looked at the stream for a moment. Wait, said Luna.
Luna flew to the Crystal Stream. She landed on a stepping stone. She looked down into the water.
Under the water, among the pebbles, there was a small silver box. Luna had put it there herself. A very long time ago.
Yes, said Luna. I remember now. She picked it up. I put it there to keep it safe. For today.
She opened the box. Inside were twelve small seeds. Each one glowed with a soft gold light. Oh, said Fern, very quietly.
Moonwood seeds, said Luna. From the first trees. For the day the old trees grow tired and need new ones beside them.
Luna gave one to each creature. One for each of them. The seeds glowed gently in their hands. Plant them this afternoon, said Luna.
They planted the seeds in a ring around the Hollow Oak. One from each of them. In the golden afternoon light.
What would have happened if you hadn't remembered? asked Wren. I did remember, said Luna. With help. She looked at all of them. That is the point.
